text_fr: 31/4. Question de l'ile comorienne de Mayotte
text_en: 31/4. Question of the Comorian island of Mayotte

text_fr: L' Assemblee generate,
text_en: The General Assembly,

text_fr: Rappe/ant que l'ensemble  du peuple de la Republique  des  Comores,  par  le  referendum  du 22  decembre 1974, a exprime a une ecrasante majorite sa  volonte  d'acceder a l'independance dans  l'unite politique et l'integrite territoriale,
text_en: Rappe/ant that the whole people of the Republic of the Comoros, by the referendum of 22 December 1974, expressed to an overwhelming majority their will to accede to independence in political unity and territorial integrity,

text_fr: Considerant que  Jes referendums  imposes  aux habitants de l'ile comorienne de Mayotte constituent une violation de la souverainete de l'Etat comorien et de son integrite territoriale,
text_en: Considering that Jes referendums imposed on the inhabitants of the Comorian island of Mayotte constitute a violation of the sovereignty of the Comorian State and its territorial integrity,

text_fr: Considerant que I' occupation par la France de l'ile comorienne  de  Mayotte  constitue  une  atteinte  fla› grante a l'unite nationale de l'Etat comorien, Membre de  l'Organisation des  Nations  Unies,
text_en: Considering that the occupation by France of the Comorian island of Mayotte constitutes an attack on the national unit of the Comorian State, Member of the United Nations,

text_fr: Considerant qu'une telle attitude de la France cons› titue une  violation des  principes des  resolutions  per› tinentes de l'Organisation des Nations  Unies, en par› ticulier  de  la  resolution  1514  (XV)  de  I' Assemblee generate,  en  date  du  14  decembre  1960,  relative a l'octroi de  l'independance aux  pays  et  aux  peuples coloniaux,  qui  garantit l'unite nationale  et  l'integrite territoriale de ces pays,
text_en: Recognizing that such an attitude by France constitutes a violation of the principles of the permanent resolutions of the United Nations, in particular resolution 1514 (XV) of 14 December 1960 on the granting of independence to colonial countries and peoples, which guarantees the national unity and territorial integrity of those countries,

items_fr: 1. Condamne Jes  referendums  du  8 fevrier  et du 11  avril 1976  organises  dans  l'ile  comorienne  de Mayotte par le Gouvemement fran9ais et les considere comme nuls et non avenus,  et rejette  :
a) Toute autre forme  de referendums ou consulta› tions  qui  pourraient etre  organises  ulterieurement en territoire comorien de Mayotte par la  France;
b)  Toute  legislation  etrangere  tendant a legaliser une  quelconque  presence  coloniale  fran9aise  en  ter› ritoire comorien de Mayotte;

2. Condamne  energiquement la  presence  de  la France a Mayotte, qui constitue une violation de l'unite nationale, de l'integrite territoriale et de la souverainete de  la  Republique independante des  Comores;

3. Demande au  Gouvemement fran9ais  de  se  re› tirer immediatement de l 'ile comorienne de Mayotte, partie  integrante  de  la  Republique  independante  des Comores, et de respecter sa souverainete;

4. Invite tous Jes  Etats Membres a apporter, indi› viduellement  et  collectivement,  une  aide  efficace a l'Etat comorien  et a cooperer avec  lui  dans  tous  Jes domaines  pour  Jui  permettre  de  defendre  et  sauve› garder son  independance,  l'integrite de  son  territoire et  sa souverainete nationale;

5. Lance un  appel a tous  Jes  Etats Membres afin qu'ils  interviennent,  individuellement  et  collective› ment,  aupres  du  Gouvemement fran9ais  en  vue  de !'amener a renoncer  definitivement a son  projet  de detacher I 'ile comorienne de Mayotte de la Republique des Comores;
6. Demande au Gouvemement fran9ais d'entamer immediatement  des  negociations  avec  le  Gouveme› ment comorien pour la mise en application des disposi› tions de la presente resolution.
items_en: 1. Condemns Jes referendums of 8 February and 11 April 1976 organized in the Comorian island of Mayotte by the French Government and considers them null and void, and rejects:
(a) Any other form of referendums or consultations which may subsequently be organized by France in Comorian territory of Mayotte;
(b) All foreign legislation aimed at legislating any French colonial presence in Mayotte's Comorian ritoire;
2. Strongly condemns the presence of France in Mayotte, which constitutes a violation of the national unit, territorial integrity and sovereignty of the independent Republic of the Comoros;
3. Calls upon the French Government to immediately withdraw from the Comorian island of Mayotte, an integral part of the independent Republic of the Comoros, and to respect its sovereignty;

4. Invites all Member States to provide, individually and collectively, effective assistance to the Comorian State and to cooperate with it in all areas to enable Jui to defend and save" keep his independence, the integrity of his territory and his national sovereign;

5. Appeals to all Member States to intervene, individually and collectively, with the French Government with a view to finally giving up its plan to detaches the Comorian island of Mayotte of the Republic of the Comoros;
6. Calls on the French Government to immediately begin negotiations with the Comorian Government for the implementation of the provisions of this resolution.

text_fr: LE TRANSKEI PRETENDUMENT INDEPENDANT ET AUTRES BANTOUSTANS
text_en: THE INDEPENDENT TRANSKEI AND OTHER BANTUSTANS

text_fr: L'Assembtee generate,
text_en: The Assemttee generate,

text_fr: Rappe/ant sa resolution 3411 D (XXX) du 28  novembre  1975  par  laquelle  elle  a  condamne  la creation de bantoustans par le regime raciste d' Afrique du  Sud,
text_en: Rappe/ant its resolution 3411 D (XXX) of 28 November 1975, in which it condemned the creation of Bantustans by the racist regime of South Africa,

text_fr: Notant que  le  regime  raciste  d'Afrique du  Sud  a proclame  l'"independance"  fictive  du  Transkei  le 26  octobre 1976,
text_en: Noting that South Africa's racist regime proclaimed Transkei's "independence" on 26 October 1976,

text_fr: Ayant examine le  rapport du  Comite  special contre l' apartheid 5 et Jes  rapports speciaux de  ce comite 6 ,
text_en: Having examined the report of the Special Committee against Apartheid 5 and the special reports of that Committee 6 ,

items_fr: 1. Condamne vigoureusement la  creation de  ban› toustans  comme  etant  une  mesure  destinee a con-
items_en: 1. Strongly condemns the creation of ban› toustans as a measure intended to be used by the

text_fr: lider la politique inhumaine d 'apartheid, a detruire l'integrite territoriale du  pays, a perpetuer la domina› tion de la minorite blanche et a deposseder la popula› tion  africaine d'Afrique  du Sud  de  ses  droits inalienables;
text_en: to link the inhuman policy of apartheid, to destroy the territorial integrity of the country, to perpetuate the dominion of the white minority and to depossess the African people of South Africa with their unalienable rights;

items_fr: 2. Rejette la  proclamation  d'"independance" du Transkei et declare qu'elle est nulle et non avenue;

3. Demande a tous les  gouvernements de refuser de  reconnaitre  sous  quelque  forme  que  ce  soit  le Transkei pretendument independant  et de  s 'abstenir d'avoir des rapports quels qu'ils soient avec le Trans› kei  pretendument  independant  ou  d'autres bantous› tans;

4. Prie tous les  Etats de prendre des  mesures ef› ficaces  pour interdire a toutes  les  personnes  physi› ques,  societes et autres institutions placees sous leur juridiction d'avoir des rapports quels qu'ils soient avec le  Transkei  pretendument  independant  ou  d'autres bantoustans.
items_en: 2. Rejects the proclamation of "independence" of the Transkei and declares that it is null and void;

3. Calls upon all Governments to refuse to recognize, in any form whatsoever, the Pretentiously Independent Transkei and to refrain from having any relationship with the Pretentiously Independent Trans› kei or other Bantus› tans;

4. Requests all States to take effective measures to prohibit all persons, companies and other institutions placed under their jurisdiction from having any relationship with the pretensively independent Transkei or other Bantustans.

text_fr: L'Assemblee generale,
text_en: The General Assembly,

text_fr: Rappe/ant ses resolutions relatives au  Fonds d'affectation  speciale des Nations Unies pour l'Afrique du  Sud,
text_en: Report/resolutions on the United Nations Special Trust Fund for South Africa,

text_fr: Ayant examine le  rapport du  Secretaire general sur le Ponds d'affectation speciale des Nations Unies pour l' Afrique  du  Sud 7 , auquel  est  annexe  le  rapport  du Conseil  d'administration  du Ponds  d'affectation speciale des Nations Unies pour 1' Afrique du Sud,
text_en: Having considered the report of the Secretary-General on the United Nations Special Assignment Ponds for South Africa, to which is annexed the report of the Board of Trustees of the United Nations Special Assignment Ponds for South Africa,

text_fr: Reaffirmant qu'une assistance  humanitaire  de  la communaute  internationale a toutes  les  personnes persecutees  en  vertu  d'une legislation  repressive  et discriminatoire en Afrique du  Sud, en Namibie et en Rhodesie du Sud est appropriee et indispensable,
text_en: Reaffirming that humanitarian assistance from the international community has all persecutes under reprehensive and discriminatory legislation in South Africa, Namibia and South Rhodesia is appropriate and indispensable,

text_fr: Profondement preoccupee par la repression massive a l'encontre des  adversaires  de !'apartheid et  de  la discrimination raciale en Afrique du Sud, y compris le massacre de nombreux manifestants pacifiques,
text_en: Deeply preoccupied by the massive repression against the opponents of apartheid and racial discrimination in South Africa, including the massacre of many peaceful demonstrators,

items_fr: 1. Exprime sa satisfaction aux gouvernements, or› ganisations et particuliers qui ont verse des contribu› tions  au  Ponds  d'affectation  speciale  des  Nations Unies pour 1' Afrique du Sud;

2. Souscrit au  pressant  appel  que  le  Conseil d'administration du  Ponds d'affectation speciale des Nations Unies pour l'Afrique du Sud a lance pour que des  contributions  plus  genereuses  soient  versees  au Ponds d'affectation speciale;
3. Felicite toutes les  organisations benevoles qui foumissent  une  assistance  humanitaire  aux  victimes de }'apartheid et de la discrimination raciale.
items_en: 1. Expresses its satisfaction to the Governments, organizations and individuals that have contributed to the United Nations Special Assignment Ponds for South Africa;
2. Endorses the urgent appeal of the Board of Trustees of the United Nations Special Assignment Ponds for South Africa for more generous contributions to the Special Assignment Ponds;
3. Congratulates all the benevolent organizations that are fraught with humanitarian assistance to the victims of apartheid and racial discrimination.

text_fr: SOLIDARITE AVEC  LES PRISONNIERS POLITIQUES SUD-AFRICAINS
text_en: SOLIDARITY WITH SOUTH AFRICAN POLITICAL PRISONERS

text_fr: L'Assemblee generale,
text_en: The General Assembly,

text_fr: Reaffirmant la legitimite  de la lutte du peuple sud› africain  pour  !'elimination  totale  de !'apartheid et l'exercice du droit a l'autodetermination par tous les habitants de l'Afrique du  Sud,
text_en: Reaffirming the legitimacy of the struggle of the South African people for the total elimination of apartheid and the exercise of the right to self-determination by all the inhabitants of South Africa,

text_fr: Notant que  le  regime  raciste  d' Afrique  du  Sud  a constamment fait fi  des resolutions de }'Organisation des Nations  U nies  lui  demandant de  mettre fin a la repression  exercee  contre  les  dirigeants  du  peuple opprime et les autres adversaires de }'apartheid et de liberer toutes les personnes emprisonnees ou soumises a des  mesures  restrictives  pour avoir  lutte  contre le systeme d'apartheid,
text_en: Noting that the racist regime of South Africa has consistently ignored the resolutions of the United Nations calling on it to put an end to the repression against the leaders of the oppressed people and other opponents of apartheid and to release all those imprisoned or subjected to restrictive measures for fighting the apartheid system,

text_fr: Gravement preoccupee par les  massacres  brutaux qui  ont  eu  lieu a Soweto  et  dans  d'autres regions d' Afrique  du  Sud,  par  l'incarceration  d'ecoliers et d'autres personnes ayant manifeste contre !'apartheid et par le fait que ces atrocites se poursuivent au mepris de la resolution 392  (1976)  du  Conseil de securite, en date du  19 juin 1976,
text_en: Gravely preoccupied by the brutal massacres in Soweto and other parts of South Africa, by the incarceration of schoolmen and other persons who have manifested themselves against apartheid and by the fact that these atrocites continue to be subjected to Security Council Resolution 392 (1976) of 19 June 1976,

text_fr: Faisant I' eloge de l'heroi'sme et des  sacrifices  du peuple sud-africain dans sa lutte pour la liberation,
text_en: Welcoming the heroism and sacrifices of the South African people in their struggle for liberation,

items_fr: 1. Condamne le  regime  raciste  d' Afrique  du  Sud pour la repression impitoyable exercee contre le  peu› ple opprime d'Afrique du Sud et les autres adversaires de l'apartheid;

2. Reaffirme sa  solidarite  avec  tous  les  Sud› Africains qui luttent contre 1' apartheid pour l'instauration d'un gouvernement  par  la  majorite  et l'exercice de leur droit a l'autodetermination et pour les  principes  consacres  dans  la  Charte  des  Nations Unies;

3. Exige la liberation immediate et inconditionnelle de toutes les  personnes emprisonnees ou soumises a des  mesures  restrictives  pour leur  participation a la lutte pour la liberation en Afrique du Sud;
4. Proclame le  11  octobre  Journee  de  solidarite avec les prisonniers politiques sud-africains;

5. Prie le  Centre  contre !'apartheid, agissant  en consultation avec le Comite special contre !'apartheid, de redoubler d'efforts pour faire connaitre la cause de tous ceux qui  sont persecutes pour leur opposition a }'apartheid en Afrique du Sud.
items_en: 1. Condemns the racist regime of South Africa for the ruthless repression against the oppressed little› ple of South Africa and the other opponents of apartheid;

2. Reaffirms its solidarity with all South Africans who are fighting against apartheid for the establishment of a government by the majority and the exercise of their right to self-determination and for the principles enshrined in the Charter of the United Nations;
3. Demands the immediate and unconditional release of all persons imprisoned or subjected to restrictive measures for their participation in the struggle for freedom in South Africa;
4. Proclaims 11 October Day of Solidarity with South African Political Prisoners;

5. Requests the Centre against Apartheid, acting in consultation with the Special Committee against Apartheid, to redouble its efforts to make known the cause of all those who are persecuted for their opposition to apartheid in South Africa.

text_fr: EMBARGO SUR LES ARMEMENTS CONTRE L'AFRIQUE ou Suo
text_en: EMBARGO ON ARMS AGAINST AFRICA or Suo

text_fr: L' Assemblee generale,
text_en: The General Assembly,

text_fr: Rappe/ant ses resolutions concernant l'embargo sur les  armements contre l'Afrique du Sud,
text_en: Rappe/t its resolutions on the arms embargo against South Africa,

text_fr: Profondement preoccupee par la situation explosive qui  regne  en Afrique  du  Sud a la suite du  massacre aveugle, par le  regime raciste, de  centaines de  mani-
text_en: Deep preoccupied by the explosive situation in South Africa following the indiscriminate massacre, by the racist regime, of hundreds of

text_fr: festants  pacifiques protestant contre !'apartheid et la discrimination raciale, y compris  de nombreux ecoliers,
text_en: peaceful festivalrs protesting against apartheid and racial discrimination, including many schoolgirls,

text_fr: Condamnant le  regime  raciste  de  I' Afrique du Sud pour sa guerre coloniale contre le peuple namibien et ses  actes  repetes  d'agression contre  la  Republique populaire d'Angola et la Republique de Zambie,
text_en: Condemning South Africa's racist regime for its colonial war against the Namibian people and its acts of aggression against the People's Republic of Angola and the Republic of Zambia,

text_fr: Notant que  le  regime  raciste  d' Afrique  du  Sud  a utilise des armes qu'il a re<;ues  de  ses allies tradition› nels,  en  particulier  les  Etats-Unis  d'Amerique,  la France, Israel, la Republique federale d' Allemagne et le  Royaume-Uni de Grande-Bretagne et d'Irlande du Nord,  ainsi  que  d'autres  pays  etrangers,  pour  la repression  en  Afrique  du  Sud  et  l'agression contre d'autres Etats,
text_en: Noting that the racist regime in South Africa has used weapons that it has received from its traditional allies, in particular the United States of America, France, Israel, the Federal Republic of Germany and the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, as well as other foreign countries, for the repression in South Africa and the aggression against other States,

text_fr: Not ant en outre que le  regime raciste d · Afrique du Sud a fourni du materiel militaire au regime minoritaire raciste  illegal  en  Rhodesie  du  Sud,  en  violation  fla› grante des resolutions du Conseil de securite,
text_en: Noted further that the racist regime of · South Africa has provided military material to the illegal racist minority regime in South Rhodesia, in violation of Security Council resolutions,

text_fr: Notant avec preoccupation l'accroissement rapide et continu du budget militaire de I' Afrique du Sud et les violations repetees de l'embargo sur Jes  armements contre I' Afrique du Sud par ses allies traditionnels, en particulier  Jes Etats-Unis,  la France,  Israel, la Republique federate d'Allemagne et le  Royaume-Uni, ainsi que d'autres pays etrangers,
text_en: Noting with concern the rapid and continuous increase in South Africa's military budget and the repeated violations of the arms embargo against South Africa by its traditional allies, in particular the United States, France, Israel, the Federal Republic of Germany and the United Kingdom, as well as other foreign countries,

text_fr: Consciente de ce que la responsabilite principale du maintien de la paix et de la securite internationales in› combe au Conseil de securite,
text_en: Aware that the primary responsibility for the maintenance of international peace and security lies with the Security Council,

text_fr: Fermement  convaincue que  des  mesures  obli› gatoires  en  vertu  du  Chapitre VII  de  la  Charte  des Nations Unies en vue d'assurer la  pleine  application de l' embargo sur Jes  armements  contre I' Afrique  du Sud sont essentielles pour prevenir une nouvelle aggra› vation de la situation,
text_en: Strongly convinced that obliging measures under Chapter VII of the Charter of the United Nations to ensure the full implementation of the arms embargo against South Africa are essential to prevent a further escalation of the situation,

text_fr: Deplorant vivement que trois membres permanents du Conseil de securite les Etats-U nis, la France et le Royaume-Uni aient jusqu'a  present empeche !'adoption de telles mesures, facilitant ainsi Ja  militari› sation de I' Afrique du Sud,
text_en: Deploring strongly that three permanent members of the Security Council of the United States, France and the United Kingdom have so far prevented the adoption of such measures, thus facilitating the establishment of South Africa,

items_fr: 1. Prie  une fois  encore le  Conseil  de  securite  de prendre des mesures d'urgence,  en  vertu du Chapitre VII de la Charte des Nations Unies, pour que tous  les  Etats  cessent  completement  de  fournir  des armes, des  munitions, des  vehicules  militaires  et des pieces  detachees  destinees  a  ces  vehicules  et  tout autre  materiel  militaire  quel  qu 'ii soit  a I' Afrique du Sud et mettent fin  a  toute cooperation  visant a  per› mettre  le  renforcement  des  forces  militaires  et  des forces de police en Afrique du Sud;
2. Prie en outre le Conseil de securite de demander notamment a tous les gouvernements :

a) D'appliquer  integralement  l'embargo  sur  Jes armements  contre  l'Afrique du  Sud,  sans  exception aucune quant au  type  d'armes, et d'interdire toutes ".iol~tions de cet embargo par des societes et des par› tlcuhers relevant de leur juridiction;
b) De  s 'abstenir d'importer du  materiel  militaire fabrique par I' Afrique du Sud ou en collaboration avec ce pays;
c)  De mettre fin a tous arrangements militaires avec le  regime raciste sud-africain et de  s 'abstenir de con› clure ou de prevoir tout arrangement de ce genre;

d)  D'interdire a  toutes  les  institutions,  agences  ou societes relevant de leur juridiction nationale de livrer a I' Afrique du Sud ou de mettre a sa disposition tous equipements, matieres fissiles ou techniques de nature a permettre au regime raciste sud-africain de se doter d'un armement nucleaire;

3. Demande aux  Gouvernements  des  Etats-Unis d'Amerique,  de  la  France  et  du  Royaume-Uni  de Grande-Bretagne et d'Irlande du Nord d'adopter une politique positive qui permette au Conseil de securite de  prendre  des  mesures  efficaces  en  vertu  du Chapitre VII de la  Charte;

4. Prie le  Comite special contre l'apartheid de con› tinuer a promouvoir  l'application  integrale de l'embargo sur les armements contre l'Afrique du  Sud et,,  a  cette  fin,  l'autorise  a  lancer  une  campagne speciale  en  vue  de  mobiliser  I' opinion  le  plus  large› ment  possible  en  faveur  de  l'embargo sur  les  arme› ments.
items_en: 1. Reiterates its request to the Security Council to take urgent measures, under Chapter VII of the Charter of the United Nations, to ensure that all States completely cease the supply of arms, ammunition, military vehicles and parts intended for these vehicles and any other military equipment in any part of South Africa and cease any cooperation aimed at strengthening the military and police forces in South Africa;
2. Further requests the Security Council, inter alia, to request all Governments:

(a) To implement in full the arms embargo against South Africa, without exception as to the type of arms, and to prohibit all measures of this embargo by companies and individuals within their jurisdiction;
(b) To refrain from importing military equipment manufactured by or in collaboration with South Africa;
(c) To put an end to all military arrangements with the South African racist regime and to refrain from construing or providing for any such arrangement;

(d) To prohibit all institutions, agencies or companies within their national jurisdiction from delivering to South Africa or making available to South Africa any equipment, material or technology that would enable the South African racist regime to acquire nuclear weapons;

3. Calls upon the Governments of the United States of America, France and the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland to adopt a positive policy that will enable the Security Council to take effective measures under Chapter VII of the Charter;

4. Requests the Special Committee against Apartheid to continue to promote the full implementation of the arms embargo against South Africa and, to this end, authorizes it to launch a special campaign to mobilise the widest possible opinion in favour of the arms embargo.

text_fr: L "Assemhlee gh1eralc,
text_en: L "Assemhlee gh1eralc,

text_fr: Rappe/am ses  condamnations  repetees  du  renfor› cement  des  relations  et  de  la  collaboration  entre  le regime raciste de I' Afrique du Sud et Israel dans les do› maines politique, militaire, economique et autres, telles qu'elles sont  formulees  dans  ses  resolutions  3151  G (XXVIII) du  14  decembre  1973,  3324  E (XXIX) du 16  decembre  1974  et  3411  G (XXX) du  lO  decembre 1975,
text_en: Repe/am its condemnations of the strengthening of relations and cooperation between the racist regime of South Africa and Israel in political, military, economic and other matters, as formulated in its resolutions 3151 G (XXVIII) of 14 December 1973, 3324 E (XXIX) of 16 December 1974 and 3411 G (XXX) of 1 December 1975,

text_fr: Reaffirmant qu'elle appuie sans reserve le  principe olympique  selon  lequel ii  ne  devrait  y  avoir  aucune discrimination  fondee  sur  la  race,  la  religion  ou }'affiliation politique,
text_en: Reaffirming its unreserved support for the Olympic principle that there should be no discrimination based on race, religion or political affiliation,

text_fr: Reconnaissant qu'il  est  important  de  boycotter, dans  le  cadre  de  la  campagne  internationale  contre l'apartheid, les equipes sportives sud-africaines selectionnees sur la base de !'apartheid,

text_fr: Regrettant que  certaines  organisations  sportives nationales et internationales et certains sportifs main› tiennent des contacts avec les organisations sportives racistes  d'Afrique du  Sud,  en  violation  du  principe olympique et des resolutions de l 'Organisation des Na› tions Unies,
text_en: Regretting that some national and international sports organisations and some sportsmen are in contact with racist sports organisations in South Africa, in violation of the Olympic principle and the resolutions of the United Nations,

text_fr: Convaincue que des mesures efficaces doivent etre prises a titre prioritaire pendant la Decennie de la lutte contre  le  racisme  et  la  discrimination  raciale  pour abolir l'apartheid dans tous les domaines,

text_fr: Notant egalement la resolution dans laquelle la cin› quieme Conference des chefs d'Etat ou de gouverne› ment des pays non alignes, reunie a Colombo du 16 au 19 aout  1976,  a  fait  sienne  la proposition concernant une convention internationale contre l'apartheid dans les  sports  et a  prie !'Organisation des  Nations  Unies d'envisager d'urgence }'elaboration de  cette conven› tion11,
text_en: Noting also the resolution in which the Fifth Conference of Heads of State or Government of non-aligned countries, meeting in Colombo from 16 to 19 August 1976, endorsed the proposal for an international convention against apartheid in sports and requested the United Nations to consider urgently the elaboration of this convention,11

text_fr: Notant en  outre que,  dans  son  rapport,  le  Comite special contre }'apartheid a  notamment recommande que 1' Assemblee  generale  etudie  la  proposition  con› cemant l'elaboration d'une convention internationale contre l'apartheid dans les sports, et qu'en attendant elle  adopte  une  declaration  sur !'apartheid dans  les sports 1 2 ,
text_en: Noting further that, in its report, the Special Committee against Apartheid recommended, inter alia, that the General Assembly should consider the proposal concerning the elaboration of an international convention against apartheid in sports, and that until then it should adopt a declaration on apartheid in sports 1 2 ,

items_fr: 1. Accueille favorablement la  proposition concer› nant  !'elaboration  d'une  convention  internationale contre !'apartheid dans les sports afin  de promouvoir l'adhesion au principe olympique de non› discrimination, de decourager les manifestations spor› tives organisees en violation de ce principe et de leur refuser tout appui;
2. Decide de creer  un  Comite  special  pour }'elaboration d'une convention  internationale  contre l'apartheid dans les sports, compose des membres ac-

text_en: the Special Committee against Apartheid and seven other Member States appointed by the President of the General Assembly on the basis of equitable geographical distribution;

items_fr: 3. Prie le  Comite special de preparer un projet de declaration sur !'apartheid dans  les  sports, a titre de mesure  interimaire,  et de  le  presenter a I' Assemblee generale lors de  sa trente-deuxieme session;

4. Prie egalement le  Comite special de prendre des dispositions preliminaires en vue de la redaction d 'une convention internationale contre !'apartheid dans  les sports  et  de  faire  rapport a ce  sujet a l' Assemblee generale !ors de  sa trente-deuxieme session;
5. Prie  instamment tous  les  Etats d'appliquer les recommandations  relatives a !'apartheid dans  Jes sports qui figurent  dans le  rapport du  Comite  special contre l'apartheid 1 \ a savoir :

a) Communiquer a toutes  Jes  organisations  spor› tives  nationales  Jes  resolutions  de !'Organisation des Nations Unies  relatives a !'apartheid dans  Jes  sports en leur demandant de prendre Jes  mesures necessaires pour Jes  appliquer:

b) N'accorder  aux rencontres sportives avec l' Afrique du Sud aucun parrainage, aucune assistance ni  aucun  encouragement  officiel,  ne  pas  organiser notamment de  receptions officielles  en l 'honneur des equipes  et  ne  pas  verser  de  dons  aux  organisations sportives, aux equipes ou aux sportifs participant a des competitions  avec  des  equipes  ou  des  sportifs  sud› africains;

c)  Refuser  d'accorder des  visas  aux  organisations sportives, aux equipes ou aux sportifs sud-africains, a !'exception des  organisations  sportives  non  raciales reconnues par le  Comite  special contre !'apartheid et les  mouvements de  liberation;
d) Refuser tout service aux organisations sportives, aux equipes ou aux sportifs qui  se rendent en Afrique du  Sud;
e) Encourager Jes organisations sportives nationales interessees a appuyer !'exclusion de  I' Afrique  du  Sud des organisations et tournois sportifs internationaux;

6. Demande aux  Etats Membres et aux organisa› tions sportives internationales de soutenir activement les  projets entrepris en collaboration avec les mouve› ments de liberation, en vue de  constituer des equipes non  raciales  qui  representeraient  vraiment  I' Afrique du  Sud.
items_en: 3. Requests the Special Committee to prepare a draft declaration on apartheid in sports as an interim measure and to present it to the General Assembly at its thirty-second session;

4. Also requests the Special Committee to take preliminary steps towards the drafting of an international convention against apartheid in sports and to report thereon to the General Assembly at its thirty-second session;
5. Urges all States to implement the recommendations relating to apartheid in Jes sports contained in the report of the Special Committee against Apartheid 1 \ to know:
(a) Communicate to all my national organizations the resolutions of the United Nations concerning apartheid in Jes sports by asking them to take the necessary measures to implement:

(b) To provide sports meetings with South Africa with no formal sponsorship, assistance or encouragement, including official receptions in honour of teams, and not to donate to sports organizations, teams or athletes participating in competitions with South African teams or athletes;

(c) Refusal to grant visas to South African sports organizations, teams or sportsmen, with the exception of non-racial sports organizations recognized by the Special Committee against Apartheid and freedom movements;
(d) Refuse any service to sports organizations, teams or athletes travelling to South Africa;
(e) Encourage my national sports organizations to support the exclusion of South Africa from international sports organizations and tournaments;

6. Calls on Member States and international sports organisations to actively support projects undertaken in collaboration with freedom movements with a view to forming non-racial teams that would truly represent South Africa.

text_en: Considering the need to further extend the activities of the Special Committee in close cooperation with specialized institutions, the movement of non-aligned countries, the Organization of African Unity, the League of Arab States, the South African Liberation Movements recognized by the Organization of African Unity, and other inter-governmental and non-governmental organizations at this crucial stage in the struggle for the total elimination of apartheid and the exercise by the South African people of their right to self-determination,

4. Autorise le  Comite  special a organiser en  1977 une Conference  mondiale pour  l'action  contre l'apartheid, conformement  aux  recommandations formulees aux paragraphes 296 a 302 de son rapport 14 ;

items_en: 1. Requests the Special Committee against Apartheid to continue and intensify its activities with a view to encouraging concerted international action in accordance with the permanent resolutions of the General Assembly;

2. Requests all organs of the United Nations that deal with the problems of decolonization to cooperate with and consult with the Special Committee with a view to ensuring the coordination of efforts;

(a) To send missions composed of members of the Special Committee and representatives of the African National Congress of South Africa and the Pan Africanist Congress of Azania to the governments of Member States, to the offices of specialized institutions and other intergovernmental and non-governmental organizations, as well as to trade union confederations, as appropriate, for the purpose of consulations with a view to promoting the national inter-party campaign against apartheid;
(b) To take appropriate measures to promote closer cooperation with the movement of non-aligned countries, the Organization of African Unity and other appropriate intergovernmental organizations;
(c) To participate in conferences dealing with apartheid;

(d) To invite the representatives of the South African national liberation movements recognized by the Organization of African Unity and those of other organizations, as well as of the experts on apartheid, for consultations on various aspects of apartheid and on measures to be taken against apartheid;
4. Authorizes the Special Committee to organize in 1977 a World Conference for Action against Apartheid, in accordance with the recommendations contained in paragraphs 296 to 302 of its report;
5. Further authorizes the Special Committee to convene an International Conference of Trade Unions against Apartheid, in accordance with paragraphs 269 to 274 of its report; 14

text_fr: L'Assemblee generale,
text_en: The General Assembly,

text_fr: Ayant examine le  rapport du Comite  special contre l'apartheid 11 et les  rapports speciaux de ce comite 18 ,
text_en: Having examined the report of the Special Committee against Apartheid 11 and the special reports of that Committee 18 ,

text_fr: Prenant  note du  soulevement  national  du  peuple opprime d' Afrique du Sud contre le regime d' apartheid,
text_en: Taking note of the national uprising of the oppressed people of South Africa against the apartheid regime,

text_fr: Scandalisee par les  massacres  et  autres  atrocites que  le  regime  raciste  d' Afrique  du  Sud  continue  de perpetrer contre des ecoliers et d'autres personnes qui manifestent paisiblement contre l'apartheid et la dis› crimination raciale,
text_en: Scandalized by the massacres and other atrocites that the racist regime of South Africa continues to perpetrate against schoolgirls and other people who peacefully protest against apartheid and racial discrimination,

text_fr: Profondement preoccupee par les activites militaires et autres du regime raciste d' Afrique du  Sud et, en particulier, par la collaboration nucleaire visant a etablir des installations nucleaires en Afrique du  Sud et a transferer la technologie nucleaire ace pays,
text_en: Deeply concerned by the military and other activities of the racist regime in South Africa and, in particular, by the nuclear collaboration aimed at the establishment of nuclear facilities in South Africa and the transfer of nuclear technology ace countries,

text_fr: Consciente que des mercenaires et les organisations dont ils  relevent operent sur le  territoire de I' Afrique du Sud et prennent part aux actes d' agression que ce pays commet contre les peoples d 'Afrique et les Etats membres de !'Organisation de l'unite africaine,
text_en: Aware that mercenaries and the organizations to which they belong operate in the territory of South Africa and take part in the acts of aggression committed by that country against the people of Africa and the member States of the Organization of African Unity,

text_fr: Convaincue que  la  situation  en  Afrique  du  Sud represente une grave menace pour la paix et la securite internationales,
text_en: Convinced that the situation in South Africa poses a serious threat to international peace and security,

text_fr: Reaffirmant que  la  politique  et  les pratiques d'apartheid constituent un  crime contre l'humanite,
text_en: Reaffirming that the policy and practices of apartheid constitute a crime against humanity,

text_en: Bearing in mind the special responsibility of the United Nations and the international community for the oppressed people of South Africa and their liberation movements, as well as for those who are imprisoned, banned or exiled as a result of their struggle against apartheid,

items_fr: 1. Proclame que  le  regime  raciste  d'Afrique du Sud est illegitime et n'a aucun droit de  representer le peuple sud-africain;

2. Reaffirme que  les  mouvements  de  liberation nationale  reconnus  par  l'Organisation  de  l'unite africaine l' African National  Congress  of  South Africa et le Pan Africanist Congress of Azania sont les  representants authentiques de l'immense majorite de la  population sud-africaine;

3. Condamne  vigoureusement le regime  raciste d'Afrique du  Sud  pour  sa politique  et ses  pratiques criminelles d'apartheid, ses  massacres  de  Noirs,  y compris  d 'ecoliers, et  sa  repression  impitoyable  de tous ceux qui  luttent contre l'apartheid;

Reconnaft en particulier que le  mepris constant temoigne  par le  regime  raciste  d'Afrique du Sud aux resolutions  de  I 'Organisation des  Nations  Unies  sur l'apartheid et la repression brutale, y compris les mas› sacres aveugles, dont ce regime continue de se rendre coupable ne laissent au  peuple opprime d'Afrique du Sud d 'autre possibilite que de recourir a la lutte armee pour faire  prevaloir ses droits legitimes;

6. Declare que la  situation existant en Afrique du Su~  du  fait  \_de  la  politique  et  des  actes  du  regime rac1ste const1tue une grave menace pour la paix et ap› pelle des mesures au titre du Chapitre VII de la Charte des Nations  Unies;
7. Exige la cessation de toute cooperation militaire et nucleaire,  sous quelque forme  que ce soit, avec  le regime raciste d'Afrique du  Sud;

8. Condamne le  regime  raciste  d'Afrique du  Sud pour  les  encouragements  qu'il apporte  aux  activites des mercenaires et des organisations dont ils  relevent sur  son  territoire  et  pour  leur  utilisation  contre  les peuples d'Afrique et les Etats membres de l'Organisation de )'unite africaine;

9. Adresse un  appel urgent a tous  les  Etats  pour qu'ils promulguent des lois faisant du recrutement, du financement,  de  l'entrainement, du  transit et du  ras› semblement  de  mercenaires  pour  le  regime  raciste d 'Afrique du  Sud sur leurs territoires un crime punis› sable  et  pour  qu'ils  interdisent  a  leurs  citoyens  de s'enroler comme mercenaires;
10. Invite les Gouvernements  des  Etats-Unis d'Amerique, de  la  France  et  du  Royaume-Uni  de Grande-Bretagne et d'Irlande du Nord, en particulier :
a) A cesser d'abuser de leur droit de veto au  Con› seil  de  securite  pour proteger  le  regime  raciste  sud› africain;
b) A permettre au Conseil de securite d'etablir qu'il existe en Afrique du  Sud une menace pour la paix et d'exercer ses responsabilites aux termes de la Charte;

c) A  ne  pas  entraver  mais a faciliter  au  contraire l'adoption d'un embargo obligatoire  sur la foumiture d'armes et d'autres mesures indispensables, en vertu du Chapitre VII de la Charte, pour faire face a la situa› tion grave qui existe en Afrique du  Sud;

11. Fait  appel a tous  les  Etats  et  organisations pour qu'ils foumissent toute !'assistance requise par le peuple opprime d'Afrique du Sud et ses mouvements de liberation nationale au cours de leur lutte legitime, compte tenu des recommandations du Comite special contre !'apartheid;

12. Invite en outre les Etats Membres et les institu› tions specialisees a apporter, par des projets communs et par une assistance financiere d'urgence, une aide au Lesotho et a d'autres pays limitrophes de I' Afrique du Sud pour  qu'ils  puissent assurer Jes moyens d'enseignement  necessaires  au  nombre  rapidement croissant d'etudiants refugies d' Afrique du  Sud;
13. Adresse un appel a tousles gouvemements qui ne l' ont pas encore fait pour qu 'ils deviennent parties a la  Convention  internationale  sur  !'elimination et  la repression du crime d'apartheid;

14. Autorise le Comite special contre !'apartheid a prendre toutes  les  mesures  appropriees  pour ~ncou› rager }'assistance qu'il a recommandee au paragraphe 264 de  son  rapport 17 , y  compris  la  cons› titution  d'un Fonds  commun  de  !'Organisation des Nations Unies et de !'Organisation de !'unite africaine qui serait finance par des contributions volontaires, et a aider }'African National Congress of South Africa et le  Pan  Africanist  Congress  of Azania a ouvrir  des bureaux au Siege de !'Organisation des Nations Unies a New York;

15. Felicite les  mouvements anti-apartheid, les mouvements de solidarite ainsi que les  autres organi› sations  non  gouvernementales  qui  ont  pris  des  me› sures contre )'apartheid et pour soutenir Jes  mouve› ments de liberation nationale sud-africains;

16. Condamne le  regime  raciste d'Afrique du Sud pour  ses  actes  d'agression contre  des  Etats  voisins independants d'Afrique qui  ont aide  les  mouvements sud-africains de liberation nationale et invite tous les gouvernements a fournir a ces  Etats,  sur  leur  de› mande, toute !'assistance necessaire pour leur defense contre l'agression;

items_en: 1. Proclaims that the racist regime of South Africa is illegal and has no right to represent the people of South Africa;

2. Reaffirms that the national liberation movements recognized by the Organization of African Unity the African National Congress of South Africa and the Pan Africanist Congress of Azania are the authentic representatives of the immense majority of the South African population;

3. Strongly condemns South Africa's racist regime for its apartheid policy and criminal practices, its massacres of blacks, including schoolgirls, and its ruthless repression of all those fighting against apartheid;

4. Reaffirms the legitimacy of the struggle waged by the oppressed people of South Africa and their liberation movements, by all means possible, to seize power and exercise their unalterable right to self-determination;

5. Recognises in particular that the constant miscarriage of South Africa's racist regime against the United Nations resolutions on apartheid and brutal repression, including the blind sacrosanct mas›, which this regime continues to make itself guilty, leaves no other possibility to the oppressed people of South Africa but to resort to the armed struggle for the exercise of their legitimate rights;

6. Declares that the situation in South Africa as a result of the policy and actions of the regime constitutes a serious threat to peace and a shovel of measures under Chapter VII of the Charter of the United Nations;
7. Demands the cessation of any military and nuclear cooperation, in any form whatsoever, with the racist regime of South Africa;

8. Condemns the racist regime in South Africa for its encouragement of the activities of mercenaries and their organizations in its territory and for their use against the peoples of Africa and the member States of the Organization of African Unity;

9. Urgently appeal to all States to enact laws that make recruitment, financing, training, transit and the removal of mercenaries for the racist regime of South Africa on their territories a punishable crime› sand and to prohibit their citizens from enrolling as mercenaries;
10. Invites the Governments of the United States of America, France and the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, in particular:
(a) To cease to abuse their veto power in the Security Council in order to protect the South African racist regime;
(b) To enable the Security Council to establish a threat to peace in South Africa and to exercise its responsibilities under the Charter;

(c) Not to impede, but to facilitate, the adoption of a mandatory embargo on the fencing of arms and other essential measures, under Chapter VII of the Charter, to deal with the serious situation in South Africa;

11. Appeals to all States and organizations to shove all the assistance required by the oppressed people of South Africa and their national liberation movements during their legitimate struggle, taking into account the recommendations of the Special Committee against Apartheid;

12. Invites Member States and specialized institutions to provide, through joint projects and emergency financial assistance, assistance to Lesotho and other countries bordering South Africa so that they can provide the necessary means of education for the rapidly growing number of refugees from South Africa;
13. Appeals to all governments that have not yet done so to become parties to the International Convention on the Elimination and Punishment of the Crime of Apartheid;

14. Authorizes the Special Committee against Apartheid to take all appropriate measures to rage the assistance recommended in paragraph 264 of its report 17 including the establishment of a Joint United Nations and Organization of African Unity Fund to be financed by voluntary contributions and to assist the African National Congress of South Africa and the Pan Africanist Congress of Azania to open offices at the Headquarters of the United Nations in New York;

15. Congratulates the anti-apartheid movements, the solidarity movements and other non-governmental organizations that have taken measures against apartheid and to support South African national liberation movements;

16. Condemns South Africa's racist regime for its acts of aggression against African-independent neighbouring States that have assisted South African national liberation movements and calls upon all governments to provide all necessary assistance for their defence against aggression;
17. Proclaims on June 16 International Day of Solidarity with the People in Struggle of South Africa and invites my Member States to commemorate this day in the most appropriate way.

items_en: I. Recommends to all Governments, organizations and individuals the Programme of Action against Apartheid annexed to this resolution;

items_fr: 1. L'abolition du regime de domination et d'exploitation racistes en  Afrique  du  Sud  et  !'aide au  peuple  sud-africain  en  vue  de l'instauration d'une societe  non  raciale  sont  devenues  l'une des preoccupations majeures de !'Organisation des Nations Unies et de la communaute intemationale.
2. L'apartheid, comme l'esclavage, doit etre elimine car ii cons› titue un  crime contre l'humanite.
3. L'apartheid doit etre elimine car ii  constitue une  insulte a la dignite  humaine  et une  grave  menace  pour  la  paix  et  la  securite intemationales.

4. L'apartheid doit  etre elimine  pour que  le  continent  africain puisse enfin etre libre apres tous les malheurs et toutes les tragedies qu'il a endures pendant des siecles, et pour qu'il lui soit enfin permis de  jouer  le  role  qui  lui  revient  de  droit  dans  les  affaires  inter› nationales.
5. L'apartheid doit etre elimine, sans quoi ii  sera impossible de faire  disparaitre  le  racisme  et  de  jeter les  bases  d 'une veritable cooperation intemationale.

6. Cela  fait  30  ans  que  !'Organisation  des  Nations  Unies s'occupe du probleme du racisme en Afrique du Sud. Elle a fait des efforts  patients pour persuader les  regimes  minoritaires  racistes de renoncer a ce triste heritage du passe et de faire le necessaire pour aboutir a une solution pacifique conforme aux principes de l'egalite humaine et de la cooperation intemationale.

7. Mais ces regimes ont fait la sourde oreille. Ils ont repondu aux revendications  pacifiques  et  justes  du  peuple  opprime  par  une repression  impitoyable  et  ont  provoque  d'immenses souffrances dans un effort desespere pour maintenir et consolider leur domina› tion  raciste.
8. Le  caractere  inhumain  des  actes  perpetres  par  le  regime d'apartheid, qui  a pris le  pouvoir en 1948,  a peu d'equivalents dans l'histoire.

9. Les Noirs, qui constituent l'ecrasante majorite de la popula› tion  du  pays,  ont  ete  constamment  humilies  et  brutalement exploites. Dans le cadre de la politique de  segregation raciale, des millions  de  personnes  ont  ete  arrachees  a  Ieur  foyer  pour  etre envoyees dans des reserves arides ou dans des ghettos urbains. Des millions d' Africains ont ete jetes en prison en vertu des lois relatives aux laissez-passer et d'autres lois racistes. Des milliers de patriotes ont ete condamnes a  de  tongues  annees de  prison OU  bien ont ete tortures ou exiles.  Des centaines de personnes ont ete massacrees !ors de manifestations pacifiques contre le  racisme.
10. Les travailleurs africains ont ete prives des droits syndicaux elementaires;  its  ont  ete  emprisonnes  OU  tues  simplement  parce qu'ils avaient commis le  "crime" de participer a des greves.

1 I. La lutte du  people  sud-africain contre ce monstre raciste a represente  une  contribution  notable  aux  efforts  que  deploie l'humanite en vue  de concretiser  les  principes  consacres  dans  la Charte  des  Nations  Unies  et dans  la Declaration  universelle  des droits de l'homme.

12. L' Assemblee generale rappelle que, dans sa resolution 3411 C (XXX) du  28  novembre  1975,  elle  a  proclame  que  !'Organisation des  Nations  Unies  et  la  communaute  internationale  ont  une
items_en: 1. The abolition of the regime of racist domination and exploitation in South Africa and aid to the people of South Africa for the establishment of a non-racial society have become one of the major concerns of the United Nations and the international community.
2. Apartheid, like slavery, must be eliminated because it is a crime against humanity.
3. Apartheid must be eliminated because ii constitutes an insult to human dignity and a serious threat to international peace and security.

4. Apartheid must be eliminated so that the African continent can finally be free after all the misfortunes and tragedies that it has endured during the centuries, and that it can finally be allowed to play its rightful role in national affairs.
5. Apartheid must be eliminated, otherwise it will be impossible to eliminate racism and lay the foundations for genuine international cooperation.

6. The United Nations has been dealing with the problem of racism in South Africa for 30 years. It has made patient efforts to persuade racist minority regimes to give up this sad heritage of the past and to do what is necessary to achieve a peaceful solution in accordance with the principles of human equality and internal cooperation.

7. But these regimes have turned a deaf ear. They have reponded to the peaceful and just demands of the oppressed people through ruthless repression and have caused immense suffering in a desperate effort to maintain and consolidate their racist dominance.
8. The inhuman character of acts committed by the apartheid regime, which took power in 1948, has few equivalents in history.

9. As part of the policy of racial segregation, millions of people have been taken to their homes to be sent to arid reserves or to urban ghettos. Millions of Africans have been thrown into prison under the laws of laissez-passer and other racist laws. Thousands of patriots have been condemned to prison years OR have been tortured or exiled. Hundreds of people have been massacred in peaceful demonstrations against racism.
10. African workers have been deprived of fundamental trade union rights; its have been imprisoned OR killed simply because they had committed the "crime" of participating in strikes.

1 I. The struggle of the South African people against this racist monster has been a significant contribution to the efforts of humanity to implement the principles enshrined in the Charter of the United Nations and in the Universal Declaration of Human Rights.
12. The General Assembly recalls that, in its resolution 3411 C (XXX) of 28 November 1975, it proclaimed that the United Nations and the international community have a

text_fr: responsabilite  particuliere  envers  le  peuple  opprime  d'Afrique du Sud et ses mouvements de liberation, ainsi qu'envers les personnes emprisonnees, frappees  d'interdiction ou  exilees  en  raison  de  leur lutte  contre !'apartheid, et  elle  a  reaffirme  sa  determination  de consacrer  une  attention  croissante  et  toutes  les ressources necessaires  pour  harmoniser  les  efforts  internationaux  en  vue  de !'elimination rapide  de l'apartheid en  Afrique  du  Sud  et  de  la liberation du peuple sud-africain.
text_en: It is particularly responsible to the oppressed people of South Africa and their liberation movements, as well as to those who are imprisoned, banned or exiled because of their struggle against apartheid, and it has reaffirmed its determination to devote increasing attention and all the resources necessary to harmonize international efforts for the speedy elimination of apartheid in South Africa and the liberation of the South African people.

items_fr: 13. L'Assemblee generate se felicite de la lutte courageuse que mene le  peuple opprime d' Afrique du Sud, sous la direction de  ses mouvements de liberation nationale, reconnus par !'Organisation de !'unite africaine, en vue d'abolir le racisme. Elle reaffirme la parfaite legitimite  de  la  Jutte  qu'il poursuit  en  vue  d'eliminer totalement I 'apartheid et de permettre a tous les  habitants de I' Afrique du  Sud d'exercer  leur  droit  a  l'autodetermination.  Elle  reaffirme  sa solidarite avec tous les  Sud-Africains qui  luttent contre !'apartheid et  pour  !'application des  principes  consacres  dans  la  Charte  des Nations  Unies  et  dans  la  Declaration  universelle  des  droits  de l'homme.

14. L' Assemblee generale condamne le regime raciste d' Afrique du Sud pour ses violations repetees et flagrantes des resolutions de !'Organisation des Nations  Unies.  Elle  denonce les  manceuvres  de ce regime, qui visent a perpetuer et a faire accepter sa monstrueuse politique d'apartheid. Elle denonce en particulier la creation de ban› toustans dans laquelle elle voit une mesure destinee a priver la popu› lation  africaine de  ses  droits  inalienables  dans tout le  pays.

15. L'Assemblee generale  declare  que  le  regime  raciste  sud› africain  est illegitime  et  n' a  pas  le  droit  de  representer  le  peuple d' Afrique du  Sud.  Elle reconnait que les  mouvements de  liberation nationale sont les representants authentiques de  la grande  majorite du  peuple  sud-africain.

16. Elle  est  convaincue  que  la  collaboration  de  certains gouvemements  et  de  certains  interets  avec  le  regime  raciste d'Afrique  du Sud  et  leur  mepris total des resolutions de !'Organisation des Nations Unies et des  revendications legitimes du peuple sud-africain ont gene les efforts visant a eliminer !'apartheid et  ont  encourage  le  regime  raciste  a  poursuivre  sa  politique  in› humaine.

I 7. Ces gouvemements et ces interets economiques ont perm is au regime raciste d' Afrique  du  Sud  de  mettre sur pied  un  appareil militaire destine a la repression interieure et a l'agression contre les Etats voisins.  Ils  ont realise des benefices s'elevant a des milliards de  dollars  grace  a  !'exploitation de  la  main-d'ceuvre africaine  en Afrique du  Sud. Ils sont pour une bonne part responsables des souf› frances endurees par le peuple sud-africain et de la menace que cette situation entraine pour la paix  intemationale.

18. L'Assemblee generale  estime  que apartheid est  un  pro› bleme  qui  conceme !'ensemble de  la  communaute  intemationale. Les gouvernements, les organisations et les peuples du monde entier doivent accroitre leur appui  a  la  lutte  legitime  que  mene le  peuple sud-africain pour la justice  et  l'exercice de  son  droit inalienable  a l'autodetermination,  sous  la  direction  de  ses  mouvements  de liberation nationale.

19. L'Assemblee  generale  charge  le  Comite  special  contre l'apartheid de  lancer,  en  cooperation  avec  les  gouvernements,  les institutions specialisees et d'autres organisations intergouvememen› tales,  ainsi  qu'avec les  syndicats,  les  eglises  et d'autres organisa› tions  non gouvemementales, une campagne internationale visant a apporter une  aide au  peuple opprime d'Afrique du  Sud  ace stade crucial et decisif de la lutte qu'il mene  pour se  liberer.
items_en: 13. The General Assembly welcomes the courageous struggle of the oppressed people of South Africa, under the leadership of their national liberation movements, recognized by the Organization of African Unity, to abolish racism. It reaffirms the perfect legitimacy of the Jutte which it pursues with a view to completely eliminating apartheid and enabling all the inhabitants of South Africa to exercise their right to self-determination. It reaffirms its solidarity with all the South Africans who fight against apartheid and for the application of the principles enshrined in the Charter of the United Nations and in the Universal Declaration of Human Rights.

14. The General Assembly condemns the racist regime of South Africa for its repeated and flagrant violations of the resolutions of the United Nations. It denounces the achievements of this regime, which aim at perpetuating and ensuring acceptance of its monstrous policy of apartheid. It declares in particular the creation of ban› toustans in which it sees a measure intended to deprive the African people of their unalterable rights throughout the country.

15. The General Assembly declares that the South African racist regime› is illegal and does not have the right to represent the people of South Africa. It recognizes that the national liberation movements are the authentic representatives of the great majority of the South African people.

16. She is convinced that the collaboration of certain governments and interests with the racist regime of South Africa and their total embodiment of the resolutions of the United Nations and the legitimate demands of the South African people have led to efforts to eliminate!' apartheid and encouraged the racist regime to pursue its human policy.

I 7. These governments and economic interests have enabled the racist regime of South Africa to set up a military apparatus designed for internal repression and aggression against neighbouring States. They have achieved benefits amounting to billions of dollars thanks to the exploitation of the African workforce in South Africa. They are largely responsible for the frances endured by the people of South Africa and for the threat that this situation brings to the internal peace.

18. The General Assembly believes that apartheid is a blemish which concerns the whole of the international community. Governments, organizations and peoples around the world must increase their support for the legitimate struggle of the South African people for justice and the exercise of their inalienable right to self-determination, under the direction of their national liberation movements.

19. The General Assembly instructs the Special Committee against Apartheid to launch, in cooperation with governments, specialized institutions and other intergovernmental organizations, as well as with trade unions, churches and other non-governmental organizations, an international campaign aimed at providing assistance to the oppressed people of South Africa at this crucial and decisive stage of the struggle to free themselves.

text_fr: I.  ACTION DES GOUVERNEMENTS
text_en: I. ACTION BY GOVERNMENTS

items_fr: 20. L'Assemblee generale demande a  tous  les  gouvemements, independamment  de  toute  divergence  de  vues,  de  s'unir dans )'action contre le crime d'apartheid et de  prendre des mesures vigou› reuses  et  concertees  pour  mettre  en  ceuvre  les  resolutions  de !'Organisation  des  Nations  Unies  visant  a  isoler  le regime d'apartheid et a preter assistance au  peuple  opprime d'Afrique du Sud et a ses mouvements de liberation jusqu'a sa liberation.
21. L' Assemblee generate demande a tous les gouvernements en particulier :
A. Relations diplomatiques,  consulaires et autres relations officielles
a) De  mettre fin  aux  relations diplomatiques,  consulaires et  au› tres  relations  officielles  avec  le  regime  raciste  sud-africain  ou  de s'abstenir d'etablir de telles  relations;
B.  Collaboration dans  /es  domaines mi/itaire et nucleaire
b) D'appliquer integralement  !'embargo  sur  les  armes  contre I' Afrique du Sud, sans exception ou reserve aucune, et, a cet egard  :

i) De  s'abstenir de  vendre  et de  livrer des armes, des  muni› tions  de  tout  type,  ainsi  que  tout  vehicule  ou  materiel destines aux forces armees et aux organisations paramilitaires d' Afrique du  Sud;

ii)  De s'abstenir de vendre et de livrer du materiel et des fourni› tures  pour  la  fabrication  et  l'entretien d'armes, de  muni› tions,  ainsi  que  de  vehicules  et  de  materiels  militaires  en Afrique du Sud;
iii) De  s'abstenir de  fournir  des  pieces  detachees  pour  Jes vehicules et le  materiel utilises par Jes  forces  armees et Jes organisations paramilitaires d' Afrique du  Sud;

iv)  De  retirer  toutes  licences  ou  brevets  octroyes  au  regime raciste sud-africain ou a des  societes sud-africaines pour la fabrication  d'armes,  de  munitions,  de  vehicules  et  de materiels militaires et de s'abstenir de delivrer des licences et brevets de  ce genre;

v)  D'interdire les investissements, ou la fourniture d'une assis› tance  technique,  pour  la  fabrication  d'armes et de  muni› tions, d'aeronefs, de navires et autres vehicules et materiels militaires en Afrique du Sud;
vi)  De  mettre fin  a tous arrangements militaires avec le  regime raciste d' Afrique  du  Sud  et de  s'abstenir de  conclure tout arrangement de ce genre;
vii)  De  s'abstenir d'assurer l'entrainement  de  membres  des forces  armees sud-africaines;
viii)  De  s'abstenir de  participer a toutes  manreuvres  militaires communes avec l'Afrique du  Sud;

ix)  D'interdire aux navires de guerre et aeronefs militaires de se rendre dans Jes  ports et aeroports  sud-africains, et aux na› vires  de  guerre  ou  aeronefs  militaires  sud-africains  de  se rendre dans leurs territoires;
x)  D'interdire les visites de militaires en Afrique du Sud et les visites de  militaires  sud-africains dans leurs pays;
xi)  Oe s'abstenir  de  proceder  a des  echanges  d'attaches militaires,  navals ou de l'air avec I' Afrique du  Sud;
xii)  De s'abstenir d'acheter tout materiel militaire  fabrique  par l'Afrique du  Sud ou en collaboration avec elle;
xiii)  De  s'abstenir  de  tout  contact  ou  communication  avec l'appareil militaire  sud-africain ou ses installations;
xiv)  De s'abstenir de toute autre forme de cooperation militaire avec I' Afrique du Sud;
xv)  D'interdire toute violation de !'embargo sur Jes armes par les societes, les  institutions ou Jes  particuliers relevant de leur juridiction;
xvi) De s 'abstenir de  toute collaboration avec I' Afrique  du  Sud dans le  domaine nucleaire;

xvii) D'interdire a toutes Jes  institutions, agences ou societes re› levant de  leur juridiction nationale de  livrer a I' Afrique  du Sud  ou  de  mettre  a sa  disposition  tous  equipements, matieres  fissiles  ou  techniques  de  nature  a permettre  au regime raciste sud-africain d'acquerir une capacite nucleaire susceptible d'etre utilisee a des fins  militaires;
items_en: 20. The General Assembly calls on all governments, regardless of any divergence of views, to unite in action against the crime of apartheid and to take vigou› reuses and concerted measures to implement the resolutions of the United Nations aimed at isolating the apartheid regime and at providing assistance to the oppressed people of South Africa and its liberation movements until its release.
21. The General Assembly calls upon all Governments in particular:
A. Diplomatic, consular and other official relations
(a) To terminate diplomatic, consular and other official relations with the South African racist regime or to refrain from establishing such relations;
B. Collaboration in the mid- and nuclear fields
(b) To implement in full the arms embargo against South Africa, without exception or reservation, and in this regard:
(i) To refrain from the sale and delivery of arms, ammunition of any kind, and any vehicles or materials intended for the armed forces and paramilitary organizations of South Africa;
(ii) To refrain from selling and delivering equipment and supplies for the manufacture and maintenance of weapons, equipment and military vehicles and equipment in South Africa;
(iii) To refrain from providing items for vehicles and equipment used by Jes forces armées and Jes organisations paramilitaires d'Afrique du Sud;

(iv) To withdraw any licences or patents granted to the South African racist regime or to South African companies for the manufacture of military weapons, ammunition, vehicles and materials and to refrain from issuing such licences and patents;
(v) To prohibit investment, or the supply of technical assistance, in the manufacture of weapons and equipment, aircraft, ships and other military vehicles and equipment in South Africa;
(vi) To put an end to all military arrangements with the racist regime in South Africa and to refrain from concluding any such arrangements;
(vii) To refrain from training members of South African armed forces;
(viii) To refrain from participating in all joint military exercises with South Africa;
(ix) To prohibit military warships and aircraft from travelling to South African ports and airports, and to South African warships or airships from travelling to their territories;
(x) To prohibit visits by military personnel to South Africa and visits by South African military personnel to their countries;
(xi) To refrain from any exchange of military, naval or air ties with South Africa;
(xii) To refrain from buying any military equipment manufactured by or in collaboration with South Africa;
(xiii) To refrain from any contact or communication with the South African military apparatus or its facilities;
(xiv) To refrain from any other form of military cooperation with South Africa;
(xv) To prohibit any violation of the arms embargo by companies, institutions or individuals under their jurisdiction;
(xvi) To refrain from any collaboration with South Africa in the nuclear field;

(xvii) To prohibit all institutions, agencies or companies within their national jurisdiction from delivering to South Africa or making available to it any equipment, material or technology of a kind that would enable the South African racist regime to acquire a nuclear capability that could be used for military purposes;

d) De  refuser les  facilites  d' atterrissage et  de  passage  a tous  les aeronefs appartenant au  regime  raciste sud-africain et  aux  societes enregistrees conformement a la legislation sud-africaine:

items_en: (c) To cease all economic cooperation with South Africa, in particular:
(i) To refrain from flirting with petroleum, petroleum products and other strategic materials in South Africa:
(ii) To refrain from providing loans, investment capital or technical assistance to South African racist regimes and registered societies in South Africa:
(iii) To prohibit the granting by banks or other financial institutions having their seat in their territory of loans to South African racist regimes or to South African societies;
(iv) To prohibit economic and financial interests within their national jurisdiction from cooperating with the South African racist regime and Jes societes registered in South Africa;
(v) To refuse tariff and other preferences for South African exports and to refrain from any encouragement or guarantee of investment in South Africa:

(vi) To take appropriate measures within the framework of institutional institutions and organisations such as the European Economic Community, the General Agreement on Tariffs and Trade, the International Monetary Fund and the International Bank for Reconstruction and Development, so that they refuse any assistance and any trade or other facilities to the South African regime;
(vii) To take appropriate measures, separately or collectively, against transnational companies working with South Africa:

(d) To refuse the landing and passage facilities to all aircraft belonging to the South African racist regime and registered companies in accordance with South African legislation:

items_en: (h) To suspend cultural, educational, sporting and other exchanges with racist regimes and South African organizations or institutions that practice apartheid;

items_en: 22. The General Assembly calls upon all specialized institutions and other intergovernmental organizations to contribute as much as possible to the international campaign against apartheid, suggesting in particular:

III. ACTION BY THE SYNDICATES, THE FRANÇAISES, THE ANTI-Apartheid MOVEMENTS' OF SOLIDARITY MOVEMENTS AND OTHER NON-GOVERNMENTAL ORGANIZATIONS

23. The General Assembly notes with satisfaction the activities of all my public organizations which declare the South African regime›, support the resolutions of the United Nations against apartheid, help the oppressed people of South Africa and mobilize public opinion against apartheid.
24. The General Assembly encourages them to consult and recommit their efforts, in cooperation with the Special Committee against Apartheid and the Centre against Apartheid, and in particular:
(a) To use their influence to persuade Governments that continue to work with the South African racist regime to cease such collaboration;
(b) To urge all Governments to implement the United Nations resolutions against apartheid;
(c) End their campaigns to boycott South African products›;
(d) To intensify my campaigns against banks and other transnational companies working with South Africa;
(e) To establish solidarity funds and provide assistance to South African liberation movements;
(f) To assist South African political refugees;
(g) To publicize the struggle for freedom in South Africa;
(h) To observe each year the International Day for the Elimination of Racial Discrimination, on 21 March, and the Toy of Solidarity with South African Political Prisoners, on 11 October.
25. In particular, the General Assembly asks the trade unions:

(a) To organize demonstrations and information campaigns among the workers to make them fully aware of the problem of apartheid and to ensure their colla› boration in the struggle in the industry against South Africa;
(b) To support boycotts of South African products coordinated in J'echelon international;
(c) Organize international trade union action to prohibit the handling of products to or from South Africa;
(d) To investigate the operations of companies with subsidiaries in South Africa;

(e) To take action in the industry of interested countries against transnational companies which refuse to recognize African trade unions in South Africa and do not comply with internationally recognized labour standards;

(f) To support morally and financially the African and non-racial trade unions of South Africa, in particular by providing legal assistance to trade unionists imprisoned or subjected to restrictive measures;
(g) To intensify my campaigns against migrant workers in South Africa;

(h) To invite my workers to refrain from carrying out arms orders to South Africa and to support without reservation those of them who refuse, out of scrupulous conscience, to work for the execution of these orders;
26. The General Assembly calls on churches and religious organizations to:
(a) Use all their influence and efforts to oppose any form of collaboration with the South African racist regime;
(b) Extend their campaigns against banks and transnational corporations working with South Africa;
(c) To provide all forms of assistance to the oppressed people of South Africa and to their liberation movements;
(d) To disseminate information on the inhumanity of apartheid and on the legitimate struggle of the oppressed people of South Africa.
27. The General Assembly calls on sports organizations and sportsmen to:
(a) Respect the Olympic principle which prohibits any discrimination based on race, religion or political affiliation;
(b) To refrain from any contact with sports organizations established on the basis of apartheid or with South African sports teams selected after racial criteria;
(c) Assist athletes and organizers of Persian sports activities in South Africa for their opposition a ('apartheid in Jes sports;
(d) Take appropriate measures to expel South African racist sports organizations from all international sports federations and competitions.

text_fr: IV. ACTION  DU CoMITE SPECIAL CONTRE L'apartheid
text_en: IV. ACTION BY THE SPECIAL COMITE AGAINST Apartheid

items_fr: 28. L 'Assemblee  generale  prie  le Co mite special contre l'apartheid, avec I'assistance du Centre contre l'apartheid, de pren› dre toutes les  mesures appropriees en vue d'encourager une action concertee contre l'apartheid de  la part des gouvernements et des organisations intergouvernementales et non gouvernementales. Elle invite  notamment  le  Comite  special  a promouvoir des  campagnes internationales coordonnees :
a) En  faveur  de  l'octroi  d'une assistance  au  peuple  opprime d'Afrique du  Sud et a ses mouvements de liberation;
b)  En faveur d'un embargo effectif sur les armes contre I' Afrique du Sud;
c)  Contre  toutes  les  formes  de  cooperation  nucleaire  avec I' Afrique  du  Sud;
d)  Contre toute collaboration des gouvernements, des banques et des  societes transnationales avec I' Afrique du Sud;
e)  Contre la propagande du regime raciste sud-africain et ses col› laborateurs;
/)  En  faveur  de  la  liberation  inconditionnelle  des  prisonniers politiques sud-africains;
g) En faveur du  boycottage des equipes sportives sud-africaines selectionnees d'apres des criteres raciaux.

29. L'Assemblee  generale invite toutes les institutions specialisees,  l'Organisation de  l'unite africaine  et autres organisa› tions intergouvernementales, ainsi  que  Jes  syndicats, Jes  eglises  et autres  organisations  non  gouvernementales  a cooperer  avec  le Comite special a ]'execution de  ce  programme d'action.
items_en: 28. The General Assembly requests the Special Committee against Apartheid, with the assistance of the Centre against Apartheid, to take all appropriate measures to encourage concerted action against apartheid by Governments and intergovernmental and non-governmental organizations, including the Special Committee on the Promotion of Coordinated International Campaigns:
(a) To provide assistance to the oppressed people of South Africa and to their liberation movements;
(b) In favour of an effective arms embargo against South Africa;
(c) Against all forms of nuclear cooperation with South Africa;
(d) Against any collaboration of Governments, banks and transnational corporations with South Africa;
(e) Against the propaganda of the South African racist regime and its workers;
(l) In favour of the unconditional release of South African political prisoners;
(g) In favour of boycotting South African sports teams selected after racial criteria.

29. The General Assembly invites all specialized institutions, the Organization of African Unity and other intergovernmental organizations, as well as trade unions, Jes eglises and other non-governmental organizations, to cooperate with the Special Committee on the Implementation of this Programme of Action.

text_fr: L' Assemblee ge nerale,
text_en: The General Assembly,

text_fr: Ayant examine le  rapport du  Comite  special contre l'apartheid 27 et les  rapports speciaux de ce comite 28 ,
text_en: Having considered the report of the Special Committee against Apartheid 27 and the special reports of that Committee 28 ,

text_fr: Not ant l' accroissement des investissements etrangers  en  Afrique  du  Sud,  lequel  encourage  et favorise  la  politique d'apartheid de  ce pays,
text_en: Noting the increase in foreign investment in South Africa, which encourages and promotes South Africa's apartheid policy,

text_fr: Accueillant comme une mesure positive la decision prise par certains gouvernements de faire en sorte qu'il ne soit plus  effectue  d'investissements en Afrique du Sud,
text_en: Welcoming as a positive step the decision taken by some Governments to ensure that investments are no longer made in South Africa,

text_fr: Considerant que  le  fait  de  mettre  un terme aux  in› vestissements etrangers en Afrique du Sud marquerait un  progres important dans la lutte contre l 'apartheid,
text_en: Recognizing that ending foreign investment in South Africa would be an important step forward in the fight against apartheid,

text_fr: Prie  instamment le  Conseil  de  securite,  lorsqu'il etudiera le  probleme de la poursuite de la lutte contre la politique d'apartheid de l' Afrique du Sud, d'envisager des  mesures  visant a empecher  que  de nouveaux  investissements  etrangers  soient  effectues en Afrique du  Sud.
text_en: Urges the Security Council to consider measures to prevent further foreign investment in South Africa when considering the continuing struggle against South Africa's apartheid policy.
